lastReleaseMessage: M7.1 multi-tenant polish: the SPA can now switch organiza...

barRaise.topFinding: Fifteenth report, ninth day with HEAD frozen at 890b493 (2026-06-13). The carried picture with one fresh wrinkle: the uncommitted cohort grew from four modified files to five, because CLAUDE.md now also carries an edit, a correct rewrite of the stale milestone-by-milestone build-order section into an accurate v1-shipped historical record. Today's canonical audit ran clean again, zero doc-vs-code drift, and re-confirmed the uncommitted DESIGN cron-trigger and milestone edits only tighten the docs toward reality. Every uncommitted change in the tree is a correct fix; the only problem is that none of them have shipped. The single HIGH is unchanged: the ship gate has failed nine consecutive cycles. The fix is still NOT a bare release.ps1, because git add -A would sweep the untracked .bak files in while *.bak is ungitignored. The clean ship is gitignore-*.bak, git rm --cached the two tracked .bak, delete the two untracked, then release. Three small steps and the verdict reverts to healthy.

actions.title: Ship the tree in the right order so the release does not self-pollute: add *.bak to .gitignore, git rm --cached scripts/discord-notify.ps1.bak scripts/release.ps1.bak, delete the two untracked .bak, then cd X:\YesAndScheduler; .\scripts\release.ps1. Lands the rebrand, the DESIGN cron-trigger/DNS corrections, the CLAUDE.md build-record rewrite, and git-guard.ps1. Closes the reliability HIGH plus the maintainability/audit-loop/ps1-stack MEDIUMs. origin/main...HEAD = 0 0, nothing external blocks it.

Pause bar-raise-scheduler-daily (or drop to twice-weekly) until HEAD moves, and reconcile .project-context.json scheduled_tasks to match. The daily run is re-deriving an effectively identical report; manual bar-raise YaS runs stay available on demand.
Rename package.json:5 description to 'Yes& Scheduler' without the U+2026 ellipsis and U+2014 em-dash, in the ship commit.
